codeReview.summary: Implementation matches the feature contract: MCP now supports --transport stdio|http, HTTP mode serves JSON-RPC via rmcp Streamable HTTP on axum, default bind is 127.0.0.1, and --remote composes through the shared DataSource path. I found one non-blocking test-quality issue that can hide regressions in HTTP tools/list verification.

issues.description: test_mcp_http_tools_list conditionally skips all assertions when the response body does not contain "tools" (lines 237-258). This allows the test to pass even when tools/list fails, reducing confidence for VAL-MCP-031 coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: worker-cli skill requires TDD (tests first), but transcript shows implementation edits to main.rs and mcp.rs before adding HTTP transport tests. skillFeedback still reports followedProcedure=true, so procedure adherence signaling is unreliable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requirement: .factory/skills/worker-cli/SKILL.md:31 ("Write tests FIRST (TDD)"). Transcript: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for b3ef3a1b-db71-4d0d-8e11-3ecb65899013 shows "Now let me update main.rs..." and mcp.rs edits before "Now let me add unit tests for the HTTP transport".

sharedStateObservations.observation: worker-cli skill contains stale instruction claiming commands/mcp.rs should be replaced entirely due to syntax errors, but the file is valid and was incrementally updated. This can misdirect future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/worker-cli/SKILL.md:24 says "commands/mcp.rs — MCP server (REPLACE entirely, has syntax error)"; current file compiles and was modified incrementally in commit 017a8fc.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared library lacks protocol-level notes for rmcp Streamable HTTP handshake details (Accept header and initialized notification), which caused extra trial-and-error during implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton includes "The HTTP server requires specific Accept headers" and "The HTTP transport needs the initialized notification" during debugging; .factory/library/architecture.md only states transport choice at a high level and does not capture these specifics.

summary: Pass. HTTP transport implementation is contract-aligned and secure-by-default (localhost binding), with stdio default preserved and remote composition intact. One non-blocking integration-test weakness could mask tools/list regressions and should be tightened in a follow-up.
